display_name: Legal Notice for Recovery of Money / Outstanding Dues
structural_summary: An advocate's demand notice on behalf of a claimant calling upon a debtor to pay outstanding dues. Layout: advocate/sender letterhead block; date; mode of dispatch (Registered Post A.D. / Speed Post / email); addressee block; subject line styled as a Legal Notice for recovery of the amount; opening recital that the notice issues under the client's instructions; numbered paragraphs reciting the transaction, the amount due, when it fell due, prior demands and the default; a demand for the principal together with interest (contractual or stated rate per annum); a compliance window of 15 or 30 days from receipt; consequences — civil suit for recovery with interest and costs, optionally a summary suit under Order XXXVII CPC; reservation of rights; signature block.
statutory_requirements:
- Identify the parties and recite the transaction precisely: the nature of the dues (loan, invoice, salary, advance, rent arrears, maintenance, deposit), the date the liability arose, and the principal amount outstanding.
- State when payment fell due, the demands and reminders made, and the debtor's default or refusal.
- DEMAND payment of the principal together with interest — at the contractual or a stated rate per annum; where none was agreed, interest may be claimed from the date of written demand [VERIFY: Interest Act, 1978].
- Give a compliance window of 15 or 30 days from receipt; this window is customary drafting practice, NOT a statutory requirement.
- On failure: civil suit for recovery of the amount with interest and costs, entirely at the debtor's risk as to costs.
- Where the debt arises from a written contract for a liquidated amount, a summary suit under Order XXXVII, Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 may be referenced.
- Issue within limitation: most money claims are barred three years from the date the debt fell due, Limitation Act, 1963 [VERIFY: applicable Article of the Schedule — varies by claim type].
- For supplies by a registered MSME, statutory interest on delayed payment may be invoked [VERIFY: MSMED Act, 2006, Ss. 15–16].
- Do not threaten criminal prosecution unless the facts genuinely disclose an offence; cite no penal provision by default.
- Record the mode of dispatch.

fields.name: debt_origin_date
fields.description: Date of the loan/invoice/advance/last salary etc. Anchored so the claim stays well within the 3-year limitation at notice date.

fields.name: payment_due_date
fields.description: Always after the origin date and always in the past (origin max -240 + offset max 180 = at least 60 days ago), keeping the default already crystallised.

fields.name: compliance_days
fields.description: Customary compliance window stated in the demand paragraph; not statutory.

fields.name: interest_rate
fields.description: Contractual or claimed rate % p.a.; when absent, the notice demands interest 'thereon' without a fixed rate.
